outcome_text: Roles and responsibilities and lines of communication related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks are documented and are clear to individuals and teams throughout the organization.
scoring_text: Skill in defining and documenting AI risk management roles, responsibilities, and communication structures, including RACI-style accountability assignment, cross-functional coordination mechanisms, and ensuring clarity of risk management expectations across organizational levels.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in defining and documenting AI risk management roles, responsibilities, and communication structures, including RACI-style accountability assignment, cross-functional coordination mechanisms, and ensuring clarity of risk management expectations across organizational levels.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-2.1 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.749, cosine: 0.721). Secondary: AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.720), AG-S-003 (AG, cosine: 0.679).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.5/10 (raw 0.749). Shared vocabulary: [accountability, ai, assignment, management, organizational, risk, structures]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [across, clarity, communication, coordination, cross, defining] vs WIDAI terms [conditions, deployment, enable, governance, knowledge, lifecycle] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.890 (P=0.884, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.721 ≈ STS 0.749 (gap 0.027). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [accountability, ai, assignment, management, organizational] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 24.4%. Shared tokens: [accountability, ai, assignment, management, organizational, risk, structures]. NIST AI RMF-only: [across, clarity, communication, coordination, cross, defining]. WIDAI-only: [conditions, deployment, enable, governance, knowledge, lifecycle].
